mission.title: Install and Configure Argo Events (Argo) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Argo Events is an event-driven workflow automation framework for Kubernetes that allows you to trigger various actions based on multiple event sources. Installing Argo Events enables you to automate workflows in response to events, enhancing your Kubernetes applications' responsiveness and interactivity.

steps.title: Add the Argo Events Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Argo Events Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the charts needed for installation.
```bash
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Argo Events using Helm
steps.description:
Next, install Argo Events in a new namespace called `argo-events` using the specified version.
```bash
helm install argo-events argo/argo-events --namespace argo-events --create-namespace --version v1.9.10
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Argo Events components are running successfully by listing the pods in the `argo-events` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Argo Events
steps.description:
Create a ServiceAccount and ClusterRoleBinding for Argo Events to ensure it has the necessary permissions.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook-sa
  namespace: argo-events
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["argoproj.io"]
  resources: ["eventbus", "eventsources", "sensors"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Argo Events webhook
steps.description:
To access the Argo Events webhook service, set up port forwarding. This allows you to interact with the service locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/events-webhook -n argo-events 8080:443
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Argo Events will show all pods in the `argo-events` namespace in a running state. You can also access the webhook service locally via port forwarding, enabling you to test event sources.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add argo https://argoproj.github.io/argo-helm
helm repo update
helm install argo-events argo/argo-events --namespace argo-events --create-namespace --version v1.9.10

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook-sa
  namespace: argo-events
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: argo-events-webhook
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets", "configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["argoproj.io"]
  resources: ["eventbus", "eventsources", "sensors"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Argo Events release from the `argo-events` namespace using Helm to remove all associated resources.
```bash
helm uninstall argo-events --namespace argo-events
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources that were created during the installation of Argo Events.
```bash
kubectl delete crd eventbuses.argoproj.io eventsources.argoproj.io sensors.argoproj.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the `argo-events` namespace to clean up any remaining resources and verify that it has been removed.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace argo-events
kubectl get namespaces | grep argo-events
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of Argo Events by exporting the existing configurations and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-events -o yaml > argo-events-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Argo Events release to the latest version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade argo-events argo/argo-events --namespace argo-events --version v1.10.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Argo Events components are running successfully after the upgrade by listing the pods in the `argo-events` namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-events
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If any pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the affected pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n argo-events
```

troubleshooting.title: Webhook service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Argo Events webhook service is not reachable, ensure that the service is running and check the port-forwarding command.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n argo-events
kubectl port-forward svc/events-webhook -n argo-events 8080:443
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter RBAC permission errors, verify that the ServiceAccount and ClusterRoleBinding are correctly set up.
```bash
kubectl get serviceaccount argo-events-webhook-sa -n argo-events
kubectl get clusterrole argo-events-webhook -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive errors related to missing CRDs, ensure that the CRDs were properly installed and are available in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl get crd
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
